description: Zod v4 .partial() keeps .default() active for absent keys, so a create schema turned into an edit schema via listingCreateSchema.partial() still fills defaults (currency='INR', negotiable=false, etc.) on a PATCH body that omitted them — silently overwriting stored values. Tests caught it: parse({}) returned 5 defaulted keys instead of {}.
resolution: Do not derive PATCH/edit schemas via .partial(). Build them explicitly from shared field-validator constants, each wrapped in .optional() with NO .default(). Keep the field validators (title, price, enums, etc.) as reused consts so create and edit still cannot drift.

content: Reuse Drizzle pgEnum values as the single source of truth to prevent DB/app enum drift: in Zod use z.enum(landType.enumValues) / z.enum(areaUnit.enumValues); for TS types use (typeof listingStatus.enumValues)[number]. Import the pgEnums from @/lib/db/schema. status.ts derives ListingStatus and its transition adjacency from listingStatus.enumValues so a column change can never silently desync the state machine, schemas, or DTO types.

name: listings-core-public-api
description: src/lib/listings (import from @/lib/listings barrel) is the shared foundation for seller CRUD, public browse, admin moderation. Exposes: status.ts canTransition/assertTransition/allowedTransitions/transitionAction/isTerminal/isPubliclyVisible + LISTING_TRANSITIONS named actions (submit/approve/reject/pause/reactivate/mark_sold/expire/renew/resubmit) + ListingTransitionError(code INVALID_TRANSITION, status 409); schema.ts listingCreateSchema/listingEditSchema (Zod v4, camelCase keys, strict()); slug.ts slugify + uniqueListingSlug(db,title,{excludeId}); types.ts ListingDTO/ListingSummary + serialize(row,media[])/serializeSummary(row,cover). Note: pg returns numeric cols (price,area,duration_s) as STRING and timestamptz as Date — serialize handles number/ISO coercion; rejectedReason is owner/admin-only, use serializeSummary for public surfaces.

name: seller-transition-guard
description: Layer role-specific transition policy on the shared listings state machine as a pure, DB-free function. src/app/api/dashboard/listings/_lib/transitions.ts resolveSellerTransition(from,to,{photoCount}): calls assertTransition() first (409 illegal), then restricts to SELLER_ACTIONS (submit/pause/reactivate/mark_sold/renew/resubmit) so legal-but-admin/worker edges like pending_review->active are 403, then requires photoCount>=1 for any move into pending_review (422). Route owns the DB (fetch status, count listing_media photos, persist); the guard owns only the decision, so it is fully unit-testable. Admin/mod builder should mirror this with an ADMIN_ACTIONS set.

content: Seller listings API uses one shared route wrapper for the spec §7 error envelope. src/app/api/dashboard/listings/_lib/http.ts handleRoute(fn) runs every handler body and mapErrorToResponse turns thrown errors into { error: { code, message } }: ZodError->400 VALIDATION_ERROR (first issue message), any Error carrying numeric status + string code (AuthenticationError 401, AuthorizationError 403, ListingTransitionError 409, local NotFoundError 404/BadRequestError 400/SellerActionError 403/PhotosRequiredError 422)->that status, else 500 INTERNAL_ERROR without leaking internals. Success is ok(data,status)-> { data }. Ownership failures return NotFoundError (404), never 403, so sellers can't probe others' listings. Other dashboard/admin builders should reuse this envelope shape.

title: Admin approve must use the named 'approve' edge, not any move into active
rationale: assertTransition(current,'active') also permits paused->active (a seller reactivate). Admin approve is strictly pending_review->active, so approveListing gates on transitionAction(current,'active')==='approve' and 409s otherwise. Using bare assertTransition would let an admin 'approve' a paused listing — a privilege/state bug a unit test caught. planRejection needs no such guard: rejected is only reachable from pending_review.

description: A Drizzle .update().set() ALWAYS fires the updated_at $onUpdate hook, so incrementing a denormalized counter (e.g. listings.view_count) via .set() silently bumps updated_at and breaks updated-at-based cache/ISR revalidation. Separately, a table-qualified SET target like set "listings"."view_count" is a Postgres syntax error.
resolution: Increment counters with raw db.execute(sql`update ${listings} set view_count = view_count + 1 where id = ${id}`): bare column names in the SET clause, and raw execute bypasses the $onUpdate hook so updated_at is untouched.

name: public-browse-keyset-fts
description: Public browse/search: keep WHERE/ORDER BY/cursor building DB-free (import type Db, pass db as a param) so it is unit-testable via query.toSQL() with no live database. Forward keyset pagination orders by (sortColumn, id ULID tiebreaker) and encodes a base64url {sort,value,id} cursor; the next-page predicate is (col OP v OR (col = v AND id OP cursorId)) with OP = lt for desc / gt for asc. Full-text search MUST render the to_tsvector('english', coalesce(title,'')||' '||coalesce(description,'')||' '||coalesce(address_text,'')) expression VERBATIM to match the listings_fts_idx GIN index, using websearch_to_tsquery for injection-safe user input. Parse untrusted query params leniently (preprocess + .catch/.default) so bad values degrade to defaults instead of 400ing a shareable URL.
